questions.question: Choose the correctly punctuated sentence:
options: Its raining, we should take an umbrella.
It’s raining; we should take an umbrella.
Its raining; we should take an umbrella.
It’s raining we should take an umbrella.
questions.explanation: The sentence needs the contraction It’s and a semicolon to join the related independent clauses.

questions.question: Choose the correct synonym for the word: ”Benevolent”
Identify the error in the sentence (if any): ”She don’t like to play cricket.”
questions.explanation: The error is don’t; with she, the correct form is doesn’t.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate word to fill in the blank: He was so tired that he walk.
questions.explanation: Hardly fits the meaning: he was so tired that he could barely walk.

questions.question: Choose the correct indirect speech: He said, ”I will go to the market tomorrow.”
options: He said he will go to the market tomorrow.
He said that he would go to the market tomorrow.
He said he would go to the market the next day.
He said that he will go to the market the next day.
questions.explanation: In indirect speech, will becomes would and tomorrow becomes the next day.

questions.question: Rearrange the following parts into a meaningful sentence: P: is one of the most valuable skills Q: being able to communicate clearly R: in both personal and professional life S: a person can develop
questions.explanation: The correct order is: being able to communicate clearly is one of the most valuable skills a person can develop in both personal and professional life.

questions.question: Rearrange the following parts into a meaningful sentence: P: when the electricity went out Q: we were watching a movie R: and had to finish it the next day S: at my friend’s house
questions.explanation: The correct order is: we were watching a movie at my friend’s house when the electricity went out and had to finish it the next day.

questions.question: Rearrange the following parts into a meaningful sentence: P: before making any major decisions Q: is wise R: to consider all possible outcomes S: and seek advice if necessary
questions.explanation: The correct order is: it is wise before making any major decisions to consider all possible outcomes and seek advice if necessary.

questions.question: Choose the correct meaning of the idiom: ”Hit the nail on the head”
questions.explanation: Hit the nail on the head means to say exactly the right thing.

questions.question: Choose the correct meaning of the idiom: ”Let the cat out of the bag”
questions.explanation: Let the cat out of the bag means to reveal a secret.

questions.question: Choose the correct meaning of the idiom: ”Once in a blue moon”
questions.explanation: Once in a blue moon means rarely.
